title: pyrus piranga
intro: Pyrus piranga, commonly known as wild cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The health benefits of Pyrus piranga include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which may aid in treating coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues. The therapeutic actions of this plant include its ability to exhibit analgesic, anti-diarrheal, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus piranga include salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, as well as flavonoids and tannins.
side_effects: Pyrus piranga can have side effects if used improperly, such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Ingesting excessive amounts of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, improper use of Pyrus piranga may also cause skin irritation, hives, and respiratory problems in some individuals. In severe cases, overconsumption of the plant can cause cardiac arrhythmias and even liver damage.
